_description: Actual match outcomes and per-question RBP results from the JTC SportsPredict platform. NEW CAMPAIGN BEST as of settlement (surpasses Mexico vs Ecuador's prior best of +154.20 RBP). 13 of 14 submitted questions beat the crowd (92.9%), the best beat-crowd rate of any match this campaign. Q15 (card during first- or second-half stoppage time) was deliberately not submitted -- see JULY9_Q15_SKIP_POSTMORTEM note below -- and is recorded here for completeness with the platform's crowd/outcome only, no our_estimate and no RBP earned or lost.

question_results.text: Will Kylian Mbappé (France, #10) score a goal (excluding own goals)?
Will Achraf Hakimi (Morocco, #2) have 1 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Validated the StatsBomb career finding (0 SOT across all 10 tracked WC18/22 appearances) over the crowd's more generous read.

The one loss of the match. The crowd (63%) was better calibrated on France's win probability than either the corrected point-in-time ordered logit (0.5525 raw) or the Smarkets-blended submission (0.60->rounded/averaged down in the final pre-submission pass to 0.55) -- worth auditing why the final submitted number drifted below the 0.60 Smarkets-adopted figure documented in the paper.

question_results.text: Will Brahim Díaz (Morocco, #10) have 1 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Biggest win of the match. PERSONAL_ZERO_OVERRIDES_TEAM_CONTEXT validated a third time this campaign on this exact player (1 SOT in his prior 5 tournament games) against a crowd that priced him near a coin flip.

question_results.text: Will Ousmane Dembélé (France, #7) have 2 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Bimodal-by-opponent-quality pattern held again -- blanked against a fourth consecutive tough opponent.

question_results.text: Will Morocco have 3 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Small win despite the outcome going against the estimate's direction -- France's defensive record (2 goals conceded in 5 games) suppressed Morocco's shot volume as flagged, and the market-corroborated lambda (3.377) kept this from being a repeat of the CAN-MOR team-SOT-ceiling disaster.

question_results.text: Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
question_results.note: The corrected point-in-time Poisson/NB total (lambda=2.299, P<=2=0.596) plus the 2022 h2h precedent (2-0) both pointed here; the crowd underpriced it.

question_results.text: Will there be 5 or more total cards?
Will the match be tied at halftime?
Will both halves have the same number of goals?
Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
Will a substitute score or assist a goal?
Will France have 5 or more corner kicks?
question_results.note: Market-corroborated lambda (6.212, P>=5=0.742) held up exactly as the convergence with the empirical estimate suggested it would.

question_results.text: Will a goal be scored after the second hydration break?
question_results.note: Settled with the corrected (0.45, lambda-scaled) reasoning in mind directionally, though the flat 0.50 actually submitted still beat the crowd's 0.52 on this NO outcome.

q15_not_submitted.text: Will a card be shown during first- or second-half stoppage time?
q15_not_submitted.note: Dropped entirely after an audit found zero prior settled instances of this exact question anywhere in the campaign's record, making the 0.28 estimate an unsupported guess rather than an evidence-based figure -- see JULY9_Q15_SKIP_POSTMORTEM.md. The actual outcome (NO) landed on the same side as the dropped 0.28 estimate, well below the crowd's 0.42, which would have been a large win (user estimated 220+ RBP) had it been submitted. This does not retroactively validate the 0.28 figure's evidentiary basis, which remained a guess at the time it was set aside -- but it does mean the decision to skip cost real, and in this instance very large, positive RBP rather than avoiding a loss.

key_lessons: New campaign-best result: +169.17 RBP, surpassing Mexico vs Ecuador's prior best of +154.20.
13/14 beat-crowd rate (92.9%) is the best beat-crowd rate of any match this campaign.

The two team-count questions carrying this match's largest identified tail risk in the absence of a market (Morocco 3+ SOT, France 5+ corners) both beat the crowd, and both had been de-risked pre-match by the close agreement between the Smarkets-implied lambda and the independently-built empirical estimate -- direct validation of that specific methodological safeguard.

The one loss (France win in regulation, -8.75) came on the DIRECT, liquid, most-trusted tier -- the crowd (63%) simply read the match-winner question better than either model output on this occasion, a reminder that DIRECT-tier trust is a strong prior, not a guarantee.

Skipping Q15 for lack of any precedent was a defensible decision on the evidence available at the time, but cost a very large positive result this time -- worth revisiting whether 'no precedent' should mean 'skip entirely' versus 'submit a deliberately conservative, clearly-flagged-as-low-confidence estimate rather than forgo the question outright'.
